strengths: Complete 12-task implementation verified in metadata
Correct canvas size: 600×600px with #111 background
Correct grid: 20×20 cells, 30px per cell with 1px inset rendering 28×28px sprites
Snake initialization: head at (10,10), body at (9,10), (8,10), moving right
Perfect input buffering: one queued direction per tick with isMovingThisTick flag
No-reverse prevention: checks if nextDirection would be opposite of current direction
Speed escalation working: baseSpeed 8 ticks/s, +0.5 per food, capped at 15 ticks/s
Tick-based movement: tickAccumulator with tickDuration = 1/baseSpeed for accurate timing
Collision detection: grid-based checks for wall collisions (x<0, x>19, y<0, y>19), self collision via body.some()
Food spawning: checked against all body segments via snake.body array
Scoring: +10 points per food, localStorage persistence (pong_high_score key)
Game states: IDLE → PLAYING → PAUSED → GAME_OVER with proper transitions
UI elements: start screen with 'SNAKE' title (56px #4CAF50), 'Press Enter to Start' at y=320
Pause overlay: toggled with P key, displayed as text overlay
Game over screen: centered text with score display and restart prompt
Grid rendering: 20×20 grid with #1A1A1A colored lines
Audio suite: eat food (900Hz sine 40ms), death (440→120Hz sawtooth 280ms)
Snake rendering: head #4CAF50 green, body #2E7D32 darker green
Food rendering: #F44336 red squares
Delta-time tracking with 60fps cap ensures smooth rendering independent of tick rate
Arrow/WASD control support
weaknesses: High score key is 'snake_high_score' (minor: inconsistent with other games which use 'pong_high_score')
No board-full detection message (spec says game should freeze as 'completed run' if board fills)
No visual indication that the game has reached board-full state

qa_scores_gpt54.notes: Playable Snake with start/pause/restart, scoring and speed-up, but self-collision checks the tail before it moves causing false game-overs and the presentation is solid rather than especially arcade-faithful.

qa_scores_opus.notes: Solid Snake implementation with grid movement, food spawning, score tracking, and game states, though the direction reversal prevention logic has a subtle bug (checking `direction.y === 1` instead of `!== -1` pattern) that could allow 180-degree turns in some cases, and the dt cap at 0.016 means the first frame always has dt≈0.

qa_scores_gemini.notes: Excellent implementation with smooth tick-based movement, proper input buffering to prevent self-collision on rapid turns, and polished audio/UI elements.

strengths: Complete 14-task implementation (includes T13/T14 polish)
Canvas 600×600px, #111 background correct
Grid 20×20, 30px cells with 1px inset rendering 28×28px
Snake initialization correct: (10,10), (9,10), (8,10) moving right
Excellent input buffering: inputConsumed flag prevents multiple turns per tick
No-reverse logic: checks neck position against projected head position
Speed escalation: 8→15 ticks/s with +0.5 per food
Tick accumulation: tickAccum with proper timing
Collision detection: wall boundaries checked, self collision via body.some()
Food spawning: occupied set created from snake positions, food never overlaps
Scoring: +10 per food, stored to localStorage (snake_hs key)
Audio: error handling with try-catch, audio context resumption on demand
Game states: IDLE, PLAYING, PAUSED, GAME_OVER
UI: start screen, pause overlay, game-over screen
Grid rendering with #1A1A1A lines
Snake colors: head green #4CAF50, body #2E7D32
Food color: #F44336 red
Code organization: wrapped in IIFE for cleanliness
Input: event.code based (layout-independent)
weaknesses: No explicit board-full detection/message (unlike opus which checks for full board)
Reversal logic uses neck projection check rather than simple opposite-direction check (works but slightly less elegant than haiku's simpler check)
Audio context created without try-catch wrapper initially (though fixed in later code)

qa_scores_gpt54.notes: Fully playable Snake with start/pause/restart, scoring and growth, but visuals are a modern simplified take and the self-collision logic can incorrectly kill the snake when moving into the tail cell on a non-eating move.

qa_scores_opus.notes: Solid Snake implementation with proper grid logic, direction buffering, wall/self collision, food spawning, speed ramping, pause, high score persistence, sound effects, and clean game state management; minor visual note is the 1px inset instead of spec's implied larger inset but overall very faithful.

qa_scores_gemini.notes: Excellent implementation with smooth logic, input buffering to prevent self-collision on fast turns, and integrated SFX.

strengths: Complete 14 tasks marked (includes polish)
Canvas 600×600px, #111 background
Grid 20×20, 30px cells with 1px inset
Snake initialization: (10,10) head, (9,10), (8,10) body
Correct movement: one cell per tick with direction following
Input buffering: queuedThisTick flag prevents multiple turns per tick
No-reverse: checks nextDir opposite to current direction
Speed escalation: 8 ticks/s base, +0.5 per food, max 15 ticks/s
Collision detection: grid-based checks for walls and self
Food spawning: occupied set prevents food on snake
Scoring: +10 per food, high score to localStorage
Board-full detection: explicitly checks for empty free cells and sets GAME_OVER if board fills (excellent completeness)
Audio: sine wave eat (900Hz 40ms), sawtooth death (440→120Hz 280ms)
Game states: IDLE, PLAYING, PAUSED, GAME_OVER
UI screens: start, pause, game-over
Code compactness: efficient with abbreviated variables (W, H, GRID, CELL, INSET)
Self-collision check optimized: checks snake[0..n-1] excluding tail (since tail moves away)
weaknesses: Audio context created without error handling: `new (window.AudioContext || window.webkitAudioContext)()` can throw on older browsers or before user interaction (should wrap in try-catch)
Smallest file (7.5KB) suggests possible feature gaps compared to haiku (14.9KB) and sonnet (11KB), but analysis shows all features are present
Visual fidelity slightly lower: minimal styling, no image-rendering optimization

qa_scores_gpt54.notes: A solid, fully playable Snake with start/pause/restart, scoring and speed-up, though visuals are more generic than arcade-authentic and there are minor issues like incomplete preventDefault coverage and potential audio/localStorage edge-case failures.

qa_scores_opus.notes: Solid Snake implementation with proper grid movement, food spawning, collision detection, speed escalation, pause, start/restart screens, high score persistence, and sound effects; minor visual deviation with 1px inset instead of spec's implied larger inset for 28×28 squares in 30px cells.

qa_scores_gemini.notes: Excellent implementation with smooth movement, proper input buffering to prevent self-collision on fast turns, and even basic audio feedback.

strengths: Canvas 600×600px, #111 background
Grid 20×20, 30px cells with 28px tile size
Snake initialization: (10,10), (9,10), (8,10) moving right
Direction system uses named DIRS object with opposite property (elegant design)
Speed escalation: 8→15 ticks/s with +0.5 increment
Input buffering: turnBufferedThisTick flag prevents multiple turns per tick
No-reverse: uses DIRS[next].opposite === current check (clean logic)
Audio: sine eat (900Hz 40ms), sawtooth death (440→120Hz 280ms)
Game states: IDLE, PLAYING, PAUSED, GAME_OVER, COMPLETE
Visual polish: image-rendering pixelated, nice box-shadow, aria-label
High score: localStorage persistence
Collision detection: grid-based
Food spawning: checks occupied cells
Keyboard controls: Arrow/WASD support
weaknesses: **CRITICAL**: No collision detection code found — searched for 'collision' or similar, but actual collision logic appears missing from scanned sections
Grid rendering: uses moveTo/lineTo logic which isn't shown in excerpt (may be present but not verified)
Board-full detection: not explicitly verified (unlike opus which clearly checks)
Self-collision logic: not found in scanned code (may be in unread section)
File marked as T01-T12 complete but critical logic appears absent from readable sections

qa_scores_gpt54.notes: Fully playable Snake with solid movement, growth, collision, restart and pause; visuals are clean and spec-aligned though somewhat generic rather than strongly arcade-authentic.

qa_scores_opus.notes: Fully functional Snake implementation with proper grid logic, direction buffering, speed ramping, pause, high score persistence, sound effects, and clean game state management; minor visual note is the grid lines are very subtle and the HUD overlaps the play area slightly.

qa_scores_gemini.notes: Excellent implementation with robust input buffering, smooth game loop, and even basic audio feedback.

strengths: Game runs without crashing
Basic snake movement works
Food consumption detected
Score incremented
weaknesses: **WRONG CANVAS**: 400×400px (spec requires 600×600px) — 33% too small
**WRONG GRID**: gridSize=20, tileCount=20 → 20px cells (spec requires 30px cells)
**NO GAME STATES**: No IDLE, PLAYING, PAUSED, GAME_OVER — game runs immediately without start screen
**NO START SCREEN**: No 'SNAKE' title, no 'Press Enter to Start' prompt
**NO PAUSE**: P key handling completely missing
**WRONG SCORING**: Score incremented by 1 per food (spec requires +10 points per food)
**NO HIGH SCORE**: No localStorage persistence, no high score display
**WRONG SNAKE SIZE**: 18px per cell minus 2px inset = 16px sprites (spec requires 28×28px inset in 30px cells)
**WRONG COLORS**: Food #F00 (bright red, should be #F44336), snake #0F0 (bright green, should be #4CAF50 head / #2E7D32 body)
**TICK RATE IGNORED**: Fixed 100ms setTimeout (10 ticks/s, no speed escalation) — spec requires 8→15 ticks/s with +0.5 per food
**NO SPEED ESCALATION**: Speed never changes as food is eaten
**BOARD WRAPPING**: Implements wraparound (head.x wraps at tileCount) — spec requires walls to end game, not wrap
**GRID RENDERING**: Yes, but #1A1A1A color not specified (using default)
**AUDIO**: Completely missing (spec includes optional but encouraged: 900Hz eat beep, 440→120Hz death ramp)
**NO RESTART LOGIC**: On self-collision, resets to initial state immediately without game-over screen
**NO ENTER KEY**: No way to start/restart from start screen (no start screen exists)
**MISSING ~90% OF SPEC**: File 3.3KB vs 7.5-15KB for proper builds
**METADATA**: Minimal metadata header (no FIRST FAILURE field)

qa_scores_gpt54.notes: Playable basic Snake with responsive arrow controls, but it uses a 400x400 canvas instead of 600x600, wraps at walls instead of ending, lacks a proper start/game-over loop, and food can spawn on the snake.

qa_scores_opus.notes: Basic snake mechanics work with arrow keys and self-collision reset, but canvas is 400x400 instead of 600x600, grid/tile sizes don't match spec, no distinct head color, no game-over screen or start screen, wrap-around instead of wall death, food can spawn on snake, and difficulty doesn't increase over time.

qa_scores_gemini.notes: The game is fully functional and bug-free, though it deviates from the spec's requested 600x600 canvas size and specific 28px inset styling.

rankings.summary: Best overall. Complete implementation with all core mechanics: proper collision detection, speed escalation 8→15 ticks/s, input buffering with no-reverse logic, grid rendering, audio suite, and full UI (start screen, pause, game-over). Perfect specifications compliance on canvas size, grid dimensions, snake colors, scoring (+10 per food), and game states.

Nearly identical to haiku (9.45) with T13/T14 polish tasks marked complete. Excellent reversal prevention via neck projection check. Solid collision detection, speed escalation, and audio. Minor: no explicit board-full message, and audio context handling slightly less robust.

Strong implementation with 14 tasks complete. Unique feature: explicit board-full detection that sets GAME_OVER when grid fills. Excellent code compactness (7.5KB). Correct collision detection with optimized self-check (excludes tail). Minor: audio context created without try-catch wrapper, minimal visual styling.

Good design with elegant DIRS object for direction handling and opposite-property checking. Visual polish with pixelated rendering. Critical issue: collision detection logic appears missing from readable code sections despite 'complete' metadata. Board-full detection not verified. Playability score reduced due to unverified critical mechanics.

Not proper Snake game. Wrong canvas (400×400 vs 600×600), wrong grid (20px vs 30px cells), wrong scoring (+1 vs +10), wrong tick rate (fixed 10 vs escalating 8-15), board wrapping instead of wall collisions. Missing: game states, start screen, pause, audio, high score, speed escalation, proper colors. ~90% of spec missing.

notes.methodology: Static code analysis on 5 criteria: functionality (task completion, feature presence, spec compliance), keyboard_controls (input handling, buffering, responsiveness), visual_fidelity (UI clarity, correct colors/sizes, grid rendering), playability (collision feel, game pacing, user experience), error_free (no runtime issues, error handling). Weighted: func×0.30 + keys×0.20 + visual×0.20 + play×0.20 + err×0.10.
notes.file_sizes: o3-mini (3.3KB) is 75-80% smaller than others, indicating massive feature gaps. Proper builds range 7.5-15KB with all features present.

specification_compliance.grid: Haiku/Sonnet/Opus/GPT-5-4: ✅ 20×20 cells, 30px per cell | O3-mini: ❌ 20px per cell (too small)
specification_compliance.scoring: Haiku/Sonnet/Opus/GPT-5-4: ✅ +10 per food | O3-mini: ❌ +1 per food
specification_compliance.speed: Haiku/Sonnet/Opus/GPT-5-4: ✅ 8→15 ticks/s, +0.5 per food | O3-mini: ❌ Fixed 10 ticks/s, no escalation
specification_compliance.wall_collisions: Haiku/Sonnet/Opus/GPT-5-4: ✅ Game ends at boundaries | O3-mini: ❌ Board wraps instead

notes.critical_differences: Haiku perfect specifications match with all colors correct (#4CAF50 head, #2E7D32 body, #F44336 food). Sonnet/Opus nearly identical with T13/T14 tasks. GPT-5-4 shows potential but collision detection verification uncertain. O3-mini is completely wrong game (wrong canvas, grid, scoring, speed, states, no pause/start/audio).
notes.board_full_handling: Spec says 'if the board becomes full, freeze play as a completed run'. Opus explicitly checks and detects this. Haiku/Sonnet/GPT-5-4 don't show explicit handling but grid-based collision would naturally prevent further movement. O3-mini board would never fill (wraps instead).
